message:
fix(session): quarantine corrupted trailing segments instead of deleting
them during index rebuild

Previously, when the session store encountered corruption during index
rebuild (oversized lines, malformed frames, or non-monotonic sequence
numbers), it would delete all subsequent segment files outright. This
was destructive — if corruption was transient or the detection was a
false positive, the deleted segments and their data were irrecoverably
lost.

The new approach renames corrupted trailing segments to .bak files
instead:
- quarantine_segment_file(): renames a segment to <name>.bak (or
  .bak.1, .bak.2, etc. if backups already exist), with up to 10,000
  suffix attempts to avoid collisions. Returns the backup path for
  logging.
- quarantine_segment_tail(): applies quarantine_segment_file() to all
  trailing segments after the corruption point, emitting a tracing::warn
  for each moved file with both the original and backup paths.
- All three corruption detection sites (oversized line, corrupted frame,
  non-monotonic sequence) now call quarantine_segment_tail() instead of
  the inline fs::remove_file loop.
- Log messages updated from "dropping" to "quarantining" to reflect the
  non-destructive behavior.

Adds two unit tests:
- quarantine_segment_file_moves_segment_to_backup: verifies basic rename
- quarantine_segment_file_uses_next_available_backup_suffix: verifies
  collision avoidance when .bak already exists

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
